title: bellevalia dubia subsp. typica
intro: Bellevalia dubia subsp. typica, also known as the lesser white sea squill, is a plant species within the Asparagaceae family, characterized by its white, bell-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the bulbs of Bellevalia dubia subsp. typica have been used in folk medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region, due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and its ability to reduce inflammation. Bellevalia dubia subsp. typica can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants.
